title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Colonel Melrose, Mr. Satterthwaite, and Mr. Quin discuss the inconsistencies regarding the death of Sir James de Witt. Melrose observes that while Laura DeWitt confessed to shooting James and Paul Danglars confessed to stabbing him, the actual cause of death was a crushed head. He concludes that both individuals provided false confessions because they mistakenly believed the other had committed the crime. Mr. Satterthwaite suggests the valet may have a motive, noting a maid's fearful reaction toward him.

Lady DeWitt is summoned and explains that she entered the study at 6:30 and found her husband dead at his desk. She had previously believed Danglars shot him because he had expressed a desire to do so. The police determine that James must have died by 6:25, suggesting the room's clock was manipulated to provide a false time. Laura DeWitt mentions that James carried a golf watch, which the Inspector retrieves. Upon examining the watch, Melrose finds the hands stopped at 6:15.

Later, during dinner at Colonel Melrose's house, the men reflect on the case. Mr. Satterthwaite notes that the suspects' confessions were based on incorrect assumptions about the method of death. The conversation shifts when Mr. Quin suggests that instead of moving clock hands forward, someone may have moved them backward to create an alibi. This realization implies that the crime might point to the valet, who claimed to be with the gamekeeper during the time in question, potentially turning the investigation around.
